version_date: 8 August 2017 (approved by WAPC; date of expiry 8 August 2027)
scope_summary: Activity centre plan for the 20.41 ha Melville District Centre on Canning Highway (parts of Attadale, Melville, Palmyra and Bicton), prepared under City of Melville Local Planning Scheme No. 6 in accordance with SPP 4.2 and the Structure Plan Framework 2015. It is the principal planning document for the centre, applying place-based development controls in the Centre C2 (R-AC0), Mixed Use (R-AC0), Residential (R60) and Public Open Space zones: building heights measured in storeys (3 to 5 by location, minimum 2 in Centre C2), nil street and lot-boundary setbacks with upper-level exceptions, landscaping/open space percentages, and a community-benefit mechanism for additional height. It targets 550 dwellings (31 dwellings per gross hectare) by 2031.

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: per location on Map 2 - Building Heights
Centre C2 and Mixed Use zones (Residential zone per R-Codes)

key_numeric_standards.topic: private open space (terrace, balcony or courtyard)

key_numeric_standards.value: nil permitted; any level above 3 storeys facing Waddell Road set back minimum 3 m from the building line of the third storey

key_numeric_standards.topic: lot boundary setback within Centre C2
key_numeric_standards.value: nil up to 4 storeys; levels above 4 storeys set back minimum 2
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: Centre C2 development adjoining Centre C2 lots

key_numeric_standards.topic: lot boundary setback to other zones
key_numeric_standards.value: nil for first storey; above 1 storey minimum 2 m to any boundary or 4 m from a northern boundary of an adjoining lot
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: Centre C2 development adjoining other zones
Mixed Use zone residential and non-residential development

key_numeric_standards.topic: lot boundary setback (non-residential, Mixed Use/Residential zones)
key_numeric_standards.value: nil up to 3 storeys adjoining Centre C2 (above: min 2 m); nil first storey adjoining Mixed Use/Residential (above: 2 m, or 4 m from northern boundary)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: non-residential development in Mixed Use and Residential zones

key_numeric_standards.topic: maximum additional building height via community benefit
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: Centre C2 zone above Map 2 heights

key_numeric_standards.value: 1 additional storey; corner element up to 10 m along the street facade
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: buildings on the corner of two gazetted roads in Centre C2

key_numeric_standards.value: R-AC0 (Centre C2 and Mixed Use); R60 (Residential zone)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: zones within the activity centre plan area
key_numeric_standards.clause_ref: Part One / Executive Summary zoning table

key_numeric_standards.value: not permitted on ground floors facing gazetted public roads
per Residential Design Codes and/or relevant local planning policy

definitions: storey (cl 4.5.2; excludes floors 50%+ below natural ground level and roof structures up to 3.0 m; mezzanines and lofts count as storeys)
land use terms adopted from Local Planning Scheme No. 6 (cl 4.4.4)

cross_references.cited: City of Melville Local Planning Scheme No. 6 (LPS6)
State Planning Policy 4.2: Activity Centres for Perth and Peel
Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015
City of Melville Local Planning Strategy

quality_flags: Two-column PDF extraction interleaves columns on many pages, mixing adjacent clauses on single lines; clause-level reading requires care
Maximum building heights per site are set on Map 2 (drawing); specific per-lot values are not recoverable from the extracted text
Manifest instrument_name says 'Structure Plan' but the instrument titles itself 'Melville District Activity Centre Plan' (same instrument, naming variance only)
